title: Introduce build-logic composite with convention plugins
description:
Extract the repeated Android build-script boilerplate into a composite-build convention-plugin setup (NiA pattern). Creates build-logic/convention/ as an includeBuild sibling with five convention plugins:

- nubecita.android.library — com.android.library, compileSdk/minSdk, JVM 17, consumer-rules.pro, sort-dependencies
- nubecita.android.library.compose — library + kotlin.plugin.compose + Compose BOM + base Compose deps
- nubecita.android.hilt — dagger.hilt.android + ksp + hilt-android runtime + compiler
- nubecita.android.feature — library + compose + hilt + :core:common + :designsystem
- nubecita.android.application — com.android.application + targetSdk + Hilt+Compose for :app

Modules declare alias(libs.plugins.nubecita.android.*) and get all the boilerplate for free. Pure refactor — zero behavior change; ./gradlew build output should match before/after.

Scope: create build-logic composite + five plugins + plugin aliases in libs.versions.toml + migrate :app, :designsystem, :core:auth to use them. Nothing else. Unblocks the feature-login PR on clean foundations.
close_reason: Merged in PR #31. Convention plugins + module migration landed.

title: Scaffold :core:auth Gradle module
description:
Create a new :core:auth Android library Gradle module that will house auth-related code (session storage, AtOAuth binding, AuthRepository, deep-link utilities). Mirrors the :designsystem scaffold pattern (nubecita-7dp). Establishes the :core:* convention for non-UI cross-cutting libraries.

Scope:
- New module at core/auth/ with com.android.library plugin
- namespace = net.kikin.nubecita.core.auth
- Empty package with placeholder; real impl lands with nubecita-nss
- settings.gradle.kts include + :app implementation(project(':core:auth'))

Acceptance: ./gradlew :core:auth:assembleDebug succeeds; :app depends on :core:auth.
close_reason: Merged in PR #28 (commit 1d2abd6); openspec change archived as 2026-04-24-add-core-auth-session-storage.

title: Hilt bindings for AtOAuth and authenticated XrpcClient
description: Extend the Hilt graph with authenticated AT Protocol bindings: provide AtOAuth (constructed with the hosted client metadata URL from nubecita-e16, the encrypted OAuthSessionStore from nubecita-nss, and the existing Ktor HttpClient) so feature code can @Inject AtOAuth to drive beginLogin/completeLogin/createClient. After a successful login, authenticated calls use AtOAuth.createClient() which returns an XrpcClient backed by a DpopAuthProvider. Key constraint: the clientMetadataUrl must be read from BuildConfig or a string resource, not hard-coded, so dev (GitHub Pages) and prod (custom domain) builds can diverge without editing the module. Design context in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-atproto-hilt-module-design.md.
close_reason: Merged in PR #32. :feature:login api/impl + :core:auth AtOAuth bindings + screenshot tests landed.

title: Establish bd-driven branch/commit workflow with helper scripts
description: Add a 'Workflow' section to CLAUDE.md documenting the convention (Conventional-Commit-prefixed branches, bd id embedded, Refs/Closes footers) and ship two shell helpers (scripts/bd-start, scripts/bd-finish) that automate the ceremony so Claude and humans both stay consistent. Unblocks clean execution of every downstream bd task.
design: Branch prefixes mirror Conventional Commit types 1:1 (feat/fix/chore/refactor/docs/test/perf/ci/build/style). Type inferred from bd type: task->chore, feature->feat, bug->fix. Slug = kebab-cased title, capped ~50 chars. Footer uses 'Refs: <id>' on WIP commits and 'Closes: <id>' on the PR body.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md has a Workflow section; scripts/bd-start and scripts/bd-finish exist, are executable, and handle the golden path (claim+branch, push+PR).
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix Copilot review findings on PR #13 (beads hooks + AGENTS.md)
description: Address 6 Copilot comments on https://github.com/kikin81/nubecita/pull/13: (1) replace hard-coded INSTALL_PYTHON paths in .beads/hooks/pre-commit and commit-msg with portable detection, (2) reorder beads integration block before exec so it actually runs, (3) deduplicate prepare-commit-msg vs scripts/git-hooks/prepare-commit-msg, (4) trim AGENTS.md to remove manual quick-ref that duplicates the BEADS INTEGRATION block.
close_reason: Applied 4 fixes: portable pre-commit/commit-msg hooks, beads block reordered before exec, prepare-commit-msg delegates to scripts/git-hooks/ canonical, AGENTS.md deduped. Commit 3376dd6, pushed to origin/f/beads.

title: Host OAuth client metadata JSON
description:
Host the AT Protocol OAuth client-metadata.json that Bluesky's authorization server fetches during PAR. Two-step approach: (1) Development: serve from GitHub Pages at https://kikin81.github.io/nubecita/oauth/client-metadata.json via docs/oauth/client-metadata.json in this repo (mirrors the atproto-kotlin sample). (2) Production: swap to a custom domain when launch-ready.

Per atproto-oauth skill: client_id must equal the hosting URL exactly; redirect_uris use single-slash (net.kikin.nubecita:/oauth-redirect — matches the app's applicationId, not the 'com.nubecita.app' placeholder originally sketched on this ticket); application_type=native; dpop_bound_access_tokens=true; token_endpoint_auth_method=none; Content-Type: application/json.

Note: the dev→prod URL swap is a one-way door that invalidates all existing sessions (client_id change). Fine while pre-launch, flag in release notes. GitHub Pages must be enabled for the repo (source: deploy from docs/ on main, or Actions workflow) — currently not configured, this task includes enabling it.
acceptance_criteria: GET https://<host>/nubecita/client-metadata.json returns valid JSON that passes atproto OAuth client validation.
close_reason: Merged in PR #30 (commit 5a3c707). GitHub Pages enabled via 'gh api POST repos/kikin81/nubecita/pages' with source main/docs. Client metadata will be live at https://kikin81.github.io/nubecita/oauth/client-metadata.json once Pages completes its first build.

title: Implement AT Protocol OAuth flow
description: Full AT Protocol OAuth: PAR, DPoP, authorization code exchange, refresh. Use atproto-auth helpers where available. Custom Tab for authorization. Redirect back via app link.
design: Client metadata JSON must be publicly hosted (see sibling task). Redirect URI registered as Android App Link with verified digital asset link.
acceptance_criteria: User can complete full OAuth against bsky.social; refresh works; tokens stored via secure-storage task; deep link handler resumes flow after redirect.
close_reason: Merged in PR #33. Custom Tab launch + redirect callback + Navigator (in :core:common) + AuthRepository.completeLogin all landed. Post-login routing decisions deferred to nubecita-30c.

title: Secure session token storage
description: Persist access/refresh JWTs across process death using EncryptedSharedPreferences (or DataStore + Tink). Survive uninstall-protection not required. Cleared on signOut.
acceptance_criteria: Tokens survive process kill; cleared on signOut; never readable from plaintext preferences on a rooted inspection.
close_reason: Merged in PR #28 (commit 1d2abd6); openspec change archived as 2026-04-24-add-core-auth-session-storage.

title: AuthRepository with app-password implementation
description: Interface AuthRepository { signIn, signOut, currentSession: Flow<Session?>, refresh() }. Default impl calls com.atproto.server.createSession via atproto-auth. Returns typed domain Session, not raw lexicon output.
acceptance_criteria: Repository is Hilt-injectable; signIn returns Result<Session>; currentSession emits null after signOut.
close_reason: Superseded — app-password auth dropped in favor of OAuth-only (see nubecita-ck0). Decision made on 2026-04-24.

title: Login screen UI with OAuth MVI flow
description: LoginContract (State/Event/Effect) + LoginViewModel extending MviViewModel, driving the atproto-oauth flow. State: handle input, isLoading, errorMessage. Events: HandleChanged, SubmitLogin, OAuthRedirectReceived, ClearError. Effects: LaunchCustomTab(authorizationUrl), NavigateToFeed, ShowError. On SubmitLogin: resolves handle, calls AtOAuth.beginLogin, emits LaunchCustomTab effect. MainActivity forwards the deep-link redirect back to the ViewModel (via nubecita-ck0's callback plumbing) which calls AtOAuth.completeLogin, then emits NavigateToFeed on success. UI uses :designsystem components — single handle TextField, primary button, inline error display. No app-password field (dropped on 2026-04-24 — nubecita-ym3 superseded by nubecita-ck0).
acceptance_criteria: User can enter a Bluesky handle, submit, see a loading state, and be redirected to the authorization server via a Chrome Custom Tab. On successful redirect back through the deep-link callback, the app navigates forward to the feed; on failure, an inline error is displayed without leaving the login screen. No app-password input anywhere on the screen.
close_reason: Merged in PR #32. The dependency on nubecita-ck0 is for the eventual Custom Tab launch + redirect callback wiring, which is ck0's scope. uf5's own scope (UI + MVI + screenshot baselines) is complete; the LaunchCustomTab effect is the documented handoff point.

title: Implement Material 3 Expressive theme with Material You dynamic color
description: Define NubecitaTheme composable inside :designsystem with Material 3 Expressive color schemes (light/dark/high-contrast), expressive typography, shape tokens reflecting the 'little cloud' aesthetic (soft corner radii), and Material You dynamic color opt-in on Android 12+ via dynamicLightColorScheme/dynamicDarkColorScheme. Expose MotionTokens for the subtle scale/fade signature.
design: Use androidx.compose.material3 1.3+ expressive APIs. Fallback palette when dynamic color unavailable. Tokens in Color.kt, Type.kt, Shape.kt, Motion.kt.
acceptance_criteria: NubecitaTheme wraps MainActivity content; sample preview renders light + dark + dynamic; typography/shape tokens consumed by at least one reusable component.
close_reason: Merged in PR #27 (commit bcb9e0e)

title: Scaffold :designsystem Gradle module
description: Create a new :designsystem module under ./designsystem/ with its own build.gradle.kts. Depends on compose-bom, material3, material3-adaptive. No app-layer dependencies. Register in settings.gradle.kts and have :app depend on it.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :designsystem:assembleDebug succeeds; :app depends on :designsystem.
close_reason: Merged in PR #27 (commit bcb9e0e)

title: Hilt module providing atproto client + auth session
description: Create a Hilt @Module (@InstallIn(SingletonComponent)) that constructs the atproto runtime client with the auth session from atproto-auth. Expose @Provides bindings for the high-level API classes used by feature code (e.g. BskyApi, AuthClient).
acceptance_criteria: App graph resolves; a throwaway test @Inject's the client and successfully calls resolveHandle for bsky.app.
close_reason: Merged via PR #23 (squash-commit 3ddf953). OpenSpec change archived to openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-23-add-atproto-hilt-module/; atproto-networking capability created in openspec/specs/.

title: Add atproto-kotlin 5.0.0 dependencies (models, runtime, oauth)
description: Add io.github.kikin81.atproto:models:5.0.0, :runtime:5.0.0, :oauth:5.0.0 to the version catalog (gradle/libs.versions.toml) and app/build.gradle.kts. Verify Maven Central resolves without credentials.
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :app:dependencies shows all three artifacts; build succeeds.
notes: Published coordinates differ from the epic's initial shorthand — artifacts live under group io.github.kikin81.atproto as at-protocol-{models,runtime,oauth} (note: oauth, not auth). Per maintainer 2026-04-21, upstream rename to io.github.kikin81:atproto-* is planned; rewire this integration once the renamed release lands.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create MviViewModel base class and marker interfaces
description: Port the slabsnap pattern into app/src/main/java/.../ui/mvi/: UiState, UiEvent, UiEffect marker interfaces plus abstract MviViewModel<S,E,F> that exposes uiState StateFlow, buffered effects Channel, abstract handleEvent(E), protected setState{reducer} and sendEffect(F).
acceptance_criteria: Files compile; base class is abstract and generic; effects use BUFFERED Channel; one sample sealed Contract.kt is added under a feature package to prove the shape.
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Authentication (OAuth)
description: Implement AT Protocol OAuth for Nubecita. App-password path was dropped on 2026-04-24 (see nubecita-ym3 closed as superseded by nubecita-ck0). Scope: OAuth flow (PAR, DPoP, authorization code exchange, refresh via atproto-oauth's AtOAuth), secure session storage, Hilt bindings for AtOAuth + authenticated XrpcClient, hosting OAuth client metadata JSON, OAuth login screen UI with deep-link callback handling, and sign-out.
design: AuthRepository abstraction hides app-password vs OAuth. Secure storage via EncryptedSharedPreferences or DataStore + Tink. OAuth client metadata JSON served from a static host (GitHub Pages or similar).
acceptance_criteria: User can log in with handle + app password; session persists across process death; sign-out clears credentials; OAuth path documented with clear follow-up tasks.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: Design system package
description: Stand up a :designsystem Gradle module with Material 3 Expressive theming (color, typography, shape, motion), Material You dynamic color support, and reusable composables (Post, Avatar, Buttons, etc.) used across feature screens. This is the single source of visual truth for Nubecita's 'little cloud' aesthetic.
design: Separate Gradle module :designsystem. Depends only on compose-bom + material3. Feature modules depend on :designsystem, never redefine theme tokens.
acceptance_criteria: :designsystem module compiles; NubecitaTheme wraps the app; dynamic color toggles on Android 12+; at least one reusable component (e.g. PostCard) is consumed by a feature screen.

title: Epic: atproto-kotlin SDK integration
description: Wire io.github.kikin81:atproto-4.9.0 (models, runtime, auth) from Maven Central into the app. Provide Hilt-bound client instances so feature code can call lexicons like app.bsky.feed.getTimeline without touching construction details.
acceptance_criteria: Gradle sync pulls models/runtime/auth artifacts; Hilt module exposes the atproto client; a smoke-test call (e.g. resolveHandle) succeeds from a throwaway test.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: MVI architecture foundation
description: Establish a reusable MVI skeleton that every ViewModel in the app must conform to. Based on the slabsnap pattern: generic MviViewModel<S:UiState, E:UiEvent, F:UiEffect> with StateFlow-backed uiState, buffered Channel for one-shot effects, abstract handleEvent, protected setState{reducer} and sendEffect helpers. Lives in a shared UI layer so feature modules depend on a single abstraction.
design: Generic abstract base class. Marker interfaces (UiState/UiEvent/UiEffect). Per-screen sealed Contract.kt file with State data class + Event/Effect sealed interfaces. Inspired by ~/code/slabsnap/app/src/main/java/us/kikinsoft/slabsnap/ui/mvi/.
acceptance_criteria: MviViewModel base class compiles and is unit-testable; one sample screen (e.g. login) extends it; doc comment explains contract pattern.

title: Diagnose cold-start 'something went wrong' after token expiry — add OAuth/HTTP/repo logs
description:
## Symptom

After leaving the app closed for a couple of hours, a cold start lands on the FeedScreen full-screen error layout: red ErrorOutline icon + 'Something went wrong / An unexpected error occurred while loading your timeline. / Try again'. Tapping 'Try again' loads the feed correctly on the first attempt.

The icon shown matches FeedError.Unknown (FeedErrorState.kt:103), not Network or Unauthenticated — so the throwable bubbling out of getTimeline is neither IOException nor NoSessionException. adb logcat shows no obvious clue.

## Hypothesis (to confirm with logs, not guess)

DPoP-Nonce + access-token expiry interaction in atproto-kotlin's DpopAuthProvider:

1. After hours idle, the persisted OAuthSession has both an expired accessToken AND a stale pdsNonce (PDS DPoP nonces rotate every few minutes).
2. First getTimeline GET → 401 + new DPoP-Nonce header.
3. DpopAuthProvider.onUnauthorized (DpopAuthProvider.kt:67-72) sees the new nonce, updates pdsNonce, returns true — short-circuiting before the refreshTokens() branch. The expired access token is never refreshed.
4. XrpcClient retries once with the same expired token + fresh nonce → 401 invalid_token.
5. XrpcClient.query() only retries once (XrpcClient.kt:58-67), so the second 401 is mapped to an XrpcException and thrown.
6. DefaultFeedRepository's runCatching surfaces it as FeedError.Unknown.

'Try again' works because the cached XrpcClient + DpopAuthProvider keep the now-correct pdsNonce, so the next 401 has no nonce to update and the onUnauthorized flow falls through to refreshTokens().

## Scope of THIS issue

Add logs only — do NOT fix. Goal: produce a logcat trace that confirms or refutes the hypothesis. Three boundaries instrumented:

1. HTTP boundary (debug-only): install Ktor Logging plugin in core/auth's HttpClientModule at LogLevel.HEADERS, gated on BuildConfig.DEBUG.
2. Auth provider boundary: Log.d in DefaultXrpcClientProvider.authenticated() — cache hit/miss + active DID.
3. Error surface boundary: .onFailure { Log.e } in DefaultFeedRepository.getTimeline() — captures throwable class + message + cause that's currently swallowed into FeedError.Unknown.

The fix (likely upstream in atproto-kotlin's DpopAuthProvider) gets a separate bd issue once logs confirm the path.

## Acceptance

- HTTP request/response logging visible in adb logcat for debug builds, hidden in release builds.
- Repro steps: kill app, wait until access token expires (~hours), cold start. Logcat shows the actual exception identity behind the Unknown error and the request/retry pattern.

title: Introduce LocalClock for deterministic relative-time previews/screenshots
description:
Screenshot tests + Compose previews that include PostCard render the relative-time label (`2h`, `5m`, etc.) by reading `Clock.System.now()` from inside `rememberRelativeTimeText`. As wall-clock advances, the label drifts and screenshot baselines fail validation. The current workaround pins `createdAt = Clock.System.now() - 2.hours` in fixtures so the *gap* is stable, but that:

1. Violates the institutional rule "no `Clock.System.now()` in screenshot tests" (the rule exists for exactly this drift class — it should be impossible by construction, not by convention).
2. Locks every fixture to a single relative-time bucket ("2h"). Tests that want to assert different buckets (`5m`, `10m`, `1h`, `1d`) can't pick them deliberately.

Standard fix: a `LocalClock` `CompositionLocal` defaulting to `Clock.System`. Fixtures use truly fixed `Instant.parse(...)` for `createdAt`. Screenshot harnesses wrap their content in `CompositionLocalProvider(LocalClock provides FakeClock(fixedNow))` and pick `fixedNow - createdAt` to land on whatever bucket they want.
acceptance_criteria:
- New `LocalClock` `CompositionLocal` in `:core:common/.../time/`, default `Clock.System`.
- `rememberRelativeTimeText`'s `clock` parameter defaults to `LocalClock.current` (no-op for production call sites).
- All `previewPosts` / `fixturePosts` helpers in `:feature:feed:impl` use truly fixed `Instant.parse(...)` for `createdAt`.
- All Compose previews + `@PreviewTest` screenshots that include PostCard wrap themselves in `CompositionLocalProvider(LocalClock provides ...)` with a fixed instant for `now`.
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:validateDebugScreenshotTest` green; running it again on a different day still green.
- `:designsystem` PostCard previews + screenshot tests can adopt the same pattern in a follow-on (this ticket scopes `:feature:feed:impl`; the PostCard suite migration is mechanical once LocalClock exists).
notes:
Bundled into PR #48 (the FeedScreen PR) so the PR doesn't ship a violation of the institutional rule. The `Clock.System.now() - 2.hours` workaround currently in the PR will be removed by this same change.

Reference: discussion in PR #48 — institutional rule is 'no Clock.System.now() in screenshot tests because of date drift.' This ticket implements the standard injection pattern that sidesteps the rule by construction.

title: Route signed-in users to Feed entry (temporary, until app shell lands)
description:
Today `MainActivity`'s reactive routing on `SessionState.SignedIn` calls `navigator.replaceTo(Main)`, which lands the user on the placeholder `MainScreen` (a list of greetings + a Refresh button). Now that `FeedScreen` is a real screen (nubecita-1d5), the post-login destination should be the feed.

This is intentionally a TEMPORARY routing — when the app-shell story (nubecita-cif epic) ships a real shell with bottom nav / top app bar / FAB, the post-login route will land on the shell, and `Feed` will be one of its tabs. Until then, routing directly to `Feed` gives the user a meaningful first screen and unblocks on-device testing.

Scope: one-line edit in `app/src/main/java/.../MainActivity.kt` plus the corresponding `Feed` import.
acceptance_criteria:
- After successful sign-in (cold or warm start), the user lands on `FeedScreen` rather than `MainScreen`.
- Sign-out behavior unchanged (still routes to `Login`).
- `MainScreen` and its VM remain in the codebase (don't delete) — a future change retires them when the app shell takes over.
- No regression in cold-start splash / auth-bootstrap timing.
notes: Part of the nubecita-cif (App shell, splash & onboarding routing) epic. Bundled into PR #48 (the FeedScreen PR) so the feed is testable end-to-end on a device the moment the PR merges.

title: Fix RelativeTimeTest Instant import drift (kotlinx.datetime → kotlin.time)
description:
`core/common/src/test/kotlin/.../time/RelativeTimeTest.kt` imports `kotlinx.datetime.Instant` but the production `formatRelativeTime()` in `RelativeTime.kt` switched to `kotlin.time.Instant` (the stdlib `Instant` promoted in Kotlin 2.x). `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` currently fails repo-wide on `:core:common:compileDebugUnitTestKotlin` with 12 'argument type mismatch' errors at lines 108–129.

Likely landed unnoticed via a Kotlin version bump after the test was last touched (commit cb589f0 `feat(core-common): add relative-time formatter`).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Check out main (or any branch with the current Kotlin version).
2. Run `./gradlew :core:common:testDebugUnitTest` (or `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest`).
3. Observe failure: `Argument type mismatch: actual type is 'kotlinx.datetime.Instant', but 'kotlin.time.Instant' was expected` repeated 12 times for RelativeTimeTest.kt lines 108–129.
acceptance_criteria:
- `./gradlew :core:common:testDebugUnitTest` passes.
- `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` passes repo-wide (no other modules affected by the same drift).
- One-line import change: `import kotlinx.datetime.Instant` → `import kotlin.time.Instant`. `TimeZone` import stays on `kotlinx.datetime`.
notes: Caught while running phase-7 verification on the add-feature-feed-screen branch (nubecita-1d5). Fix bundled into the same PR rather than spun out as a separate cycle — one-line change, unblocks the repo-wide test signal.

title: Persist nav back stack across process death (rememberNavBackStack migration)
description:
`DefaultNavigator` currently backs the Nav3 back stack with `mutableStateListOf<NavKey>(start)` (core/common/src/main/kotlin/.../navigation/DefaultNavigator.kt). That state is in-memory only — on process death the back stack reboots from the start destination, so any user-perceived position (deep-linked PostDetail, scrolled-deep Feed, mid-flow Compose draft, etc.) is lost on relaunch.

Per the Nav3 docs (multiple-backstacks recipe: 'persists config changes and process death'), the canonical fix is `rememberNavBackStack`-backed storage with `@Serializable` NavKeys. Our current keys are already compatible: `Splash`, `Main`, `Login`, `Feed` are all `@Serializable data object`. Future keys with arguments (PostDetail with AT-URI, Profile with DID) will need `@Serializable data class` — a per-feature discipline, not a blocker for this change.

This is the prerequisite for relying on `FeedScreen`'s back-nav scroll retention (added in nubecita-1d5) to also survive process death. Today the existing `rememberSaveableStateHolderNavEntryDecorator` + `rememberViewModelStoreNavEntryDecorator` wiring (app/src/main/java/.../Navigation.kt) handles config-change retention correctly; only process death is the gap.
design:
Architectural trade-off requires a decision before implementation. Two viable paths:

(A) **Move back-stack ownership into the MainNavigation composable** using `rememberNavBackStack(Splash)`. `DefaultNavigator` becomes a thin shim that exposes its operations (`goTo`, `goBack`, `replaceTo`) over a Compose-side reference handed in at composition time, OR the Navigator interface migrates to a `@Composable`-context provider that MainActivity reads via `LocalNavigator.current`. This is the canonical Nav3 shape per the recipes but breaks our current pattern of `@Inject lateinit var navigator: Navigator` in MainActivity reactive collectors.

(B) **Keep Hilt-singleton `DefaultNavigator` and add SavedStateHandle-backed persistence inside the impl.** Inject `SavedStateHandle` (via a `@HiltViewModel`-scoped wrapper, or via a custom `@Singleton` saved-state surface), serialize the `SnapshotStateList<NavKey>` to JSON on each mutation, restore on construction. Preserves all existing call sites; trades architectural purity for compatibility.

Lean: A is the documented Nav3 idiom. B preserves MainActivity's existing reactive routing without restructuring. Decide in implementation step 1.

Out of scope: ViewModel-level `SavedStateHandle` plumbing for per-screen state survival (e.g., `FeedState.posts`). That's a separate per-feature concern; the back stack is the only thing this ticket persists.
acceptance_criteria:
- Back stack survives process death: scroll-deep + kill process via `adb shell am kill` + relaunch lands on the deep destination, not the start.
- Existing reactive routing in `MainActivity` (`sessionStateProvider.state.collect → navigator.replaceTo`) keeps working with no behavior change for cold-start auth resolution.
- Existing per-entry `rememberSaveable` state retention (config change, push/pop) continues to round-trip — no regression.
- No source-level breakage at `Navigator` call sites in `:feature:*:impl` modules (login, feed); if the chosen path requires interface changes, callers update in the same change.
- nav3-core stays at 1.1.1 (no version bump required by the migration itself).
- Test coverage: at minimum a unit test of the serialize/restore round-trip; an instrumented smoke test using `Don't keep activities` developer-option semantics (or `ActivityScenario`'s state-machine APIs) if practical.
notes:
Path-A reference: navigation-3 skill multiple-backstacks recipe — uses `rememberNavigationState` at the composable layer with `rememberNavBackStack(key)` per top-level route.

Path-B reference: standard Hilt + SavedStateHandle pattern. Note that `SavedStateHandle` is normally VM-scoped; using it inside a `@Singleton` requires injecting it from a top-level retained surface (e.g., `MainActivity`'s `savedStateRegistryOwner`) and threading it explicitly.

Risk: NavKey serialization regression. Once the back stack is persisted, every NavKey type that lands on it must be `@Serializable` and remain backward-compatible across versions (renames break restore). Add a lint or unit test that enumerates all NavKey subclasses and asserts `@Serializable` presence.

Coordination: tasks in nubecita-1d5 (`add-feature-feed-screen`) currently mention `SavedStateHandle` for process-death scroll retention. Once this ticket lands, that language can simplify — `rememberSaveable` + persisted back stack covers both config change and process death without per-screen `SavedStateHandle` plumbing for the scroll-position case specifically.

Reference: openspec change `add-feature-feed-screen` design audit findings (the audit flagged this gap and recommended filing this follow-on).

title: Add accessibility labels to PostStat icons (especially the share button)
description:
PostStat icons currently use Icon(contentDescription = null) per the convention that the visible count text is the announced label. That's correct for reply/repost/like (where the count is shown), but the share button passes count = "" so TalkBack announces nothing — the user has no way to discover the action exists.

Fix shape:
  - Pass an accessibility-only label per stat: replyLabel/repostLabel/likeLabel/shareLabel as parameters to PostStat, default to null (Icon(contentDescription = null) preserved).
  - PostCard wires R.string.postcard_action_reply / _repost / _like / _share strings as the labels.
  - For the share button specifically, contentDescription becomes the SOURCE of the announcement since count is empty.

Also worth checking: when active = true on like/repost, the announcement should reflect the active state ("Unlike" / "Undo repost" rather than "Like" / "Repost"). Add separate strings + flip based on active.

Surfaced during the hardcoded-strings audit on PR #42 — most user-facing strings got localized, but PostStat doesn't expose ANY strings (the count is its only label, and counts aren't translatable text). Accessibility is the under-the-hood concern this surfaces.

Also worth: the icon labels are currently brand-implicit (heart = like, repeat = repost). On voice-only navigation the action verbs need to be explicit.
acceptance_criteria: PostStat exposes a label parameter per call site; PostCard supplies localized labels via R.string.postcard_action_*; share-button TalkBack announcement reads the action name; like/repost active state announces the inverse action; new strings in :designsystem/strings.xml; existing screenshot baselines unchanged (no visual diff).

title: PostCard quoted post (record) embed (app.bsky.embed.record)
description:
Render Bluesky quoted-post embeds in PostCard. Schema (per app.bsky.embed.record lexicon): a referenced app.bsky.feed.defs#postView containing the quoted post's full author + text + embed.

UX shape: nested card-within-card. Common implementations use a compact density (smaller avatar, no action row, single-line truncated text) or full density (same as parent PostCard). Choose at implementation time — likely compact, with tap-to-expand or tap-to-navigate-to-detail.

Scope decision context: filed under nubecita-6rh as v2 — lands after PostCard v1 (text + images) and v1.1 (external) ship, AND after feed scrolling is verified at 120Hz on a known-good device. Recursive composable + new layout decisions make this the highest-architectural-risk embed.

Implementation notes:
- Mapper sealed type adds Record(quotedPost: QuotedPostUiModel) variant.
- QuotedPostUiModel reuses author + text + (one level of) embed but with compact/disabled action row.
- Recursive PostCard problem: limit nesting to 1 level (a quoted post inside a quoted post just renders 'Quoted post (tap to view)' chip).
- Tap behavior: navigate to PostDetail screen (separate ticket — likely a future epic) or open the quoted post in-feed scroll.

Decision doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-postcard-embed-scope-v1.md
acceptance_criteria: Record(quotedPost) variant added to embed sealed type; nested card composable renders at compact density (≤ parent height); recursive nesting is bounded to 1 level (deeper renders a 'View thread' chip); tap navigates to a destination (post detail or in-feed scroll — pick at implementation); unit test covers nested + bounded-recursion cases.

title: PostCard external link card embed (app.bsky.embed.external)
description:
Render Bluesky external link card embeds in PostCard. Schema (per app.bsky.embed.external lexicon):
- uri: String (the linked URL)
- title: String (page title, may be empty)
- description: String (page description, may be empty)
- thumb: Blob? (optional preview image; blob ref to fetch via getBlob)

UX shape: Material 3 surface tile with rounded corners, thumb on left or top (decide based on aspect ratio), title (titleMedium), description (bodySmall, ellipsized at 2 lines), domain footer (labelSmall). Tap opens via custom tab (use existing CustomTabsIntent path from MainActivity OAuth flow as the pattern).

Scope decision context: filed under nubecita-6rh as v1.1 — next embed type to land after PostCard v1 (text + images) ships. Common in news/article-sharing feeds.

Implementation notes:
- Mapper sealed type adds External(uri, title, description, thumbUrl?) variant.
- Thumb blob ref: AT Protocol blobs need to be turned into a CDN URL (typically https://cdn.bsky.app/img/feed_thumbnail/plain/{did}/{cid}@jpeg or similar — verify against atproto-kotlin or a working post fixture).
- Reuse NubecitaAsyncImage for the thumb.
- Domain extraction: Uri.parse(uri).host with www. stripped.

Decision doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-postcard-embed-scope-v1.md
acceptance_criteria: External(uri, title, description, thumbUrl?) variant added to embed sealed type; PostCardExternalEmbed composable renders the card with M3 Expressive surface treatment; tap launches CustomTabsIntent; missing-thumb case renders gracefully (text-only card); unit test covers the mapper for fixture posts with and without thumb.

title: Decision: Bluesky embed types supported in PostCard v1
description:
Bluesky posts may carry one of five embed types (or none):
1. app.bsky.embed.images — 1-4 images, each with alt text, aspect ratio, blob ref
2. app.bsky.embed.external — link card (uri, title, description, optional thumb)
3. app.bsky.embed.record — quoted post (a referenced post view)
4. app.bsky.embed.video — single video clip with playlist (.m3u8) URL
5. app.bsky.embed.recordWithMedia — composition of #record + #images or #video

Each adds material UI work and dependency surface (video needs Media3/ExoPlayer, quoted posts need recursive PostCard rendering, link cards need OG-preview-style rendering). Shipping all five as v1 inflates feed scope significantly.

Recommendation:
- v1: text + #images. Highest-value, lowest-effort. Covers majority of feed posts.
- v1.1: #external. Modest effort, common in news/article-sharing feeds.
- v2: #record (quoted posts). Recursive composable + new layout work.
- v3: #video, #recordWithMedia. ExoPlayer integration is a multi-day item.

Decide here, document in PostCard composable's KDoc which embed types it handles vs. degrades to an 'Unsupported embed' chip, and file follow-on bd issues for each deferred embed type so they're not lost.

Blocks PostCard (nubecita-w0d) — embed handling is the bulk of PostCard surface area.
design: DECISION: PostCard v1 renders text + app.bsky.embed.images only. All other embed types degrade to an 'Unsupported embed' chip (deliberate degradation, not error). Roadmap: v1.1=external (nubecita-aku), v2=record (nubecita-6vq), v3=video+recordWithMedia (nubecita-xsu, nubecita-umn). Full rationale + UX shape + migration triggers in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-postcard-embed-scope-v1.md.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in this issue (or a docs/superpowers/specs/ note); follow-on bd issues filed for each deferred embed type with priority + linkage; PostCard implementation respects the decided scope.
close_reason: Merged in PR #36 (commit b41029c). Decision: PostCard v1 = text + app.bsky.embed.images only; deferred embeds degrade to Unsupported chip. Roadmap + 4 follow-on tickets (aku/6vq/xsu/umn) filed.

title: Rich-text renderer for AT Protocol post facets
description:
AT Protocol post records carry a facets array describing annotations on the post text:
- app.bsky.richtext.facet#mention (DID payload — resolve to handle for display, navigate to profile)
- app.bsky.richtext.facet#link (URI payload — open via custom tab or external browser)
- app.bsky.richtext.facet#tag (tag payload — navigate to search/hashtag feed)

Need a Compose-side renderer: (text: String, facets: List<Facet>, onClick: (FacetTarget) -> Unit) -> AnnotatedString. Style mentions/links/tags with primary color + appropriate semantics. ClickableText (or pointerInput w/ TextLayoutResult) for tap handling.

CRITICAL CORRECTNESS NOTE: facet byteStart/byteEnd are UTF-8 byte offsets, but Kotlin strings are UTF-16. Emoji, accented characters, and non-Latin text break any naive text.substring(byteStart, byteEnd) slice. The renderer must convert byte ranges to char ranges via a UTF-8 round-trip (text.toByteArray(Charsets.UTF_8).copyOfRange(...).toString(Charsets.UTF_8) approach, or a precomputed byte->char index map). Without this, the first emoji in a post breaks every facet boundary after it.

Lives in :designsystem (reused by feed, post detail, profile bio, notifications).

Blocks PostCard (nubecita-w0d).
acceptance_criteria: Composable renders String + List<Facet> as AnnotatedString with mention/link/tag styling; tap callbacks fire FacetTarget sealed type with payload; emoji-bearing test post (e.g. "hi 👋 @alice.bsky.social") renders mention boundary at correct char offset; unit test explicitly covers UTF-8<->UTF-16 boundary case.
notes: Upstream design discussion filed: kikin81/atproto-kotlin#20 (proposes a 3-tier API in two new artifacts at-protocol-compose + at-protocol-compose-material3). If the upstream helper ships in time, supersede this ticket and consume the SDK helper directly. If we need PostCard sooner, implement nubecita-side using the same shape (typed sealed parent + LinkAnnotation.Url + escape-hatch builder) so the migration to the upstream helper is a near-drop-in.
close_reason: Superseded by upstream atproto-kotlin v5.1.0 :compose-material3, adopted in PR #40 (commit 9f3e16d). Filed upstream as kikin81/atproto-kotlin#20 (closed/done) — instead of building a colocated facet renderer, PostCard will call rememberBlueskyAnnotatedString from the upstream lib.

title: Wire Coil for Compose image loading
description:
Coil is listed in CLAUDE.md as pending. Posts carry author avatars and (per the chosen embed scope) embedded images, neither of which can render without an image pipeline. coil-compose's AsyncImage is the Compose integration surface.

Decisions to make at implementation time:
- Where does the dep live? Default: add to :designsystem so every Compose image flows through one config (placeholder, error fallback, crossfade). Alt: standalone :core:image module if non-Compose call sites appear.
- Centralize an ImageLoader Hilt @Singleton with the chosen disk-cache size, OkHttp client, and crossfade defaults. Components consume a project-defined wrapper (e.g. NubecitaAsyncImage) rather than passing config per call site.
- Coil 3 vs 2: Coil 3 is current and KMP-friendly. Default to 3 unless a transitive blocks it.

Blocks PostCard (nubecita-w0d) — avatars and image embeds need this.
acceptance_criteria: coil-compose dep wired in :designsystem; ImageLoader provided as Hilt @Singleton; one canonical wrapper composable consumed by at least one component; placeholder/error states match Material 3 Expressive surface tokens.
close_reason: Merged in PR #35 (commit 4947525). coil-core + coil-network-okhttp wired in :app, coil-compose in :designsystem; ImageLoader Hilt @Singleton with 25% memory + 50MB disk cache; NubecitaApplication implements SingletonImageLoader.Factory; NubecitaAsyncImage + NubecitaAvatar primitives shipped.

title: Add DefaultAuthRepository unit tests covering refresh-on-success contract
description:
Per Copilot review on PR #34, DefaultAuthRepository's new behaviors aren't unit-tested:
- completeLogin success → SessionStateProvider.refresh() invoked
- signOut success → AtOAuth.logout() called + SessionStateProvider.refresh() invoked
- AtOAuth throws → Result.failure carrying the exception, NO refresh
- Log.e fires on failure (assertable via android.util.Log shadowing)

The block today: AtOAuth is a final concrete class from atproto-oauth — can't subclass for fakes. Two implementation paths:

(a) Ktor MockEngine: ~80 lines of test setup canned-responding to AtOAuth's PAR + token endpoint requests. Exercises real AtOAuth code paths. testImplementation(io.ktor:ktor-client-mock).

(b) Test-only OAuthFlowOps interface seam in :core:auth wrapping the three AtOAuth methods we use (beginLogin/completeLogin/logout). Production binding wraps real AtOAuth; tests use a fake. ~20 lines refactor + 30 lines test.

Lean toward (a) — keeps DefaultAuthRepository's current shape, tests via the seam Bluesky's actual auth server provides (HTTP). Decide at implementation time.

Also add coverage for DefaultAuthRepository.beginLogin which we wired up under nubecita-4g7 but never tested either.

title: Adaptive two-pane list-detail layout via material3-adaptive
description: Once the home feed + PostCard + profile primitives exist, wire a window-size-class-aware list-detail layout using androidx.compose.material3.adaptive so tablets / foldables / landscape phones show feed + selected post/profile side-by-side, and compact widths collapse to single-pane navigation. Uses NavigationSuiteScaffold for primary nav adaptivity and ListDetailPaneScaffold (or TwoPaneScaffold) for the feed↔detail split. Scope is shell-level adaptive scaffolding; does not own theme tokens (those live in :designsystem per nubecita-mi3).

title: Add Konsist architecture tests to guard repo conventions
description:
Adopt Konsist (com.lemonappdev:konsist:0.17.3, Apache-2.0, ~1.6k stars, Kotlin Foundation grant winner, JUnit-based) as a testImplementation dependency so codebase conventions are enforced as unit tests instead of living only in CLAUDE.md. Konsist reads the project's Kotlin AST and exposes a fluent API for asserting package layout, naming, annotations, and inter-layer dependencies.

CAVEATS
- Latest release is 0.17.3 (2024-12-08), ~15 months old as of 2026-04-22. Main branch has a 2026-01 commit, so maintenance continues — but version-drift risk is real. Revisit the choice at the 18-month mark if no new releases land.
- Must run sonatype check on pkg:maven/com.lemonappdev/konsist@0.17.3 before the coord lands.
design:
Wiring
- Add konsist = "0.17.3" under [versions] and konsist = { module = "com.lemonappdev:konsist", version.ref = "konsist" } under [libraries] in gradle/libs.versions.toml.
- Add testImplementation(libs.konsist) to app/build.gradle.kts.
- Place rules under app/src/test/java/net/kikin/nubecita/konsist/ so the existing testDebugUnitTest task picks them up automatically — no new Gradle task, no new source set. Keep the package name literally 'konsist' so scanning excludes are easy (Konsist can skip its own test files via withoutPackage).

Starter rule set — encode conventions already documented in CLAUDE.md and the dependency-injection / mvi-foundation specs
1. ViewModelRulesTest — every class whose name ends in 'ViewModel' extends MviViewModel or androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel; every @HiltViewModel uses @Inject constructor(...); no field injection anywhere.
2. MviFoundationRulesTest — classes implementing UiState are data classes; UiEvent and UiEffect implementations are sealed interfaces / sealed classes; every VM exposes a StateFlow<*> property named 'state' (or inherited from MviViewModel).
3. PackageLayoutRulesTest — classes ending in 'ViewModel', 'Screen', 'State', 'Event', 'Effect' live under net.kikin.nubecita.ui..; @Module / @InstallIn classes live under net.kikin.nubecita.data.. (until a dedicated di/ package is introduced).
4. HiltRulesTest — every @Module is an object (matching the AtProtoModule / DataModule pattern) or abstract class (for @Binds); @Module classes are annotated @InstallIn.
5. ComposableRulesTest — functions annotated @Composable that take a ViewModel parameter obtain it via hiltViewModel() from androidx.hilt.navigation.compose, not androidx.lifecycle.viewmodel.compose.viewModel().

Non-goals for v1
- No clean-architecture layer assertion (domain/data/presentation) — the project isn't structured that way yet; revisit once feature modules land.
- No detekt / ktlint duplication — spotless+ktlint already cover formatting; Konsist stays in the 'architectural invariants' lane.
- No enforcement of immutable-list types on UiState fields — that's a Compose perf concern Konsist can check via type-name matching, but the kotlinx-collections-immutable API isn't rigidly named; defer until MVI base has ≥3 consumers to generalize the rule shape.
- No Konsist CI-only gating — tests run in the same testDebugUnitTest invocation the existing unit tests use; CI already covers that path.
acceptance_criteria:
- gradle/libs.versions.toml contains konsist version + library alias; app/build.gradle.kts declares testImplementation(libs.konsist).
- Sonatype check on pkg:maven/com.lemonappdev/konsist@0.17.3 is clean (not malicious, not end-of-life, policy compliant); flagged to user if not.
- At least 5 Konsist test classes under app/src/test/java/net/kikin/nubecita/konsist/ covering the rule groups listed in design.
- ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest passes, with the Konsist tests included in the run (grep the test report for 'konsist' package output).
- ./gradlew spotlessCheck sortDependencies lint stays clean.
close_reason: abandoned: upstream maintenance stalled — see LemonAppDev/konsist#1856 (December 2025 'is this still maintained?' discussion, no maintainer response 4 months later) and the 15-month gap since the 0.17.3 release (2024-12-08). PR #24 closed unmerged. Future convention-enforcement work will evaluate detekt (+ ForbiddenImport / custom rules) and/or ArchUnit-Kotlin when multi-module splits arrive.

title: Android instrumented tests via ReactiveCircus/android-emulator-runner
description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the app/src/androidTest/ source set on an emulator using ReactiveCircus/android-emulator-runner. Triggers (must NOT run on every PR — instrumented tests are slow and resource-heavy): (1) push to main, (2) workflow_dispatch for manual runs, (3) PR label-gated (e.g., run-instrumented) so reviewers can opt in when the risk warrants it. First payload: migrate the existing MainScreenTest.kt under this gate, and add a @HiltAndroidTest that @Injects XrpcClient and calls IdentityService.resolveHandle(bsky.app) end-to-end — satisfies the deferred 'live @Inject' half of nubecita-ipa's original acceptance criterion. Design context in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-atproto-hilt-module-design.md.
notes:
Add splash + auth-gated routing instrumented scenarios (added under add-splash-routing):
- Cold-start with no session: app launches, system splash visible, lands on Login (not Main).
- Cold-start with session present: seed the OAuthSessionStore via test setup, launch app, splash visible, lands on Main.
- Rotation during splash: rotate device while SessionStateProvider is still Loading; verify no crash, no flicker, lands on correct destination after rotation.
- Mid-session signOut: simulate AuthRepository.signOut() success while on Main; verify SessionStateProvider transitions to SignedOut and MainActivity reactively replaceTo's Login.
- Cold-start with corrupted store: tamper with the encrypted DataStore file; verify load() returns null, splash routes to Login (not crash).

title: Add pre-push hook to verify screenshot test compiles
description: Our screenshotTest source set occasionally drifts out of sync with main source edits. CI catches it with :app:validateDebugScreenshotTest, but that's after push. Add a pre-push stage to .pre-commit-config.yaml that runs :app:compileDebugScreenshotTestSources so compile breakage is caught locally before the remote round-trip.
close_reason: Closed

title: Adopt Turbine + MockK + JUnit 5 in the test stack
description: Foundation change add-mvi-foundation (nubecita-aew) landed with JUnit 4 + kotlinx-coroutines-test only. As the app grows, Turbine (for Flow assertions), MockK (idiomatic Kotlin mocking), and JUnit 5 (parameterized tests, nested contexts, @RegisterExtension) will remove boilerplate from VM tests. Out-of-scope for add-mvi-foundation; tracked here.
close_reason: Landed via PR #43 (squash 3dd7221) — openspec change add-feature-feed-foundation. nubecita-4u5 = FeedContract + FeedViewModel + repository + mapper + Hilt nav wiring + XrpcClientProvider; nubecita-e1a = JUnit 5 + Turbine + MockK + :core:testing migration (subsumed by the test-stack overhaul in the same PR).

title: Top-level Navigation Compose graph
description: Single-activity Navigation Compose graph with routes: splash, onboarding, login, feed. Typed route objects (Kotlin serialization). Nested graphs reserved for future profile/thread branches.
acceptance_criteria: Deep link to feed works when authed; back from login exits app (not onboarding); graph is unit-testable via TestNavHostController.
close_reason: Superseded — single-activity Nav 3 graph with typed NavKeys (kotlinx-serialization), Login route, and the EntryProviderInstaller @IntoSet pattern for cross-module destinations all shipped under add-feature-login (PR #32) and add-oauth-flow (PR #33). Splash route + auth-gated routing covered by nubecita-30c; Onboarding + Feed routes deferred to their own feature modules.

title: Splash screen with auth-gated routing
description: Use androidx.core.splashscreen SplashScreen API. setKeepOnScreenCondition { authBootstrapInFlight }. AuthBootstrap coroutine checks first-install flag + current session, then routes to Onboarding / Feed / Login via NavController.navigate with popUpTo(splash, inclusive).
acceptance_criteria: Cold start: no flicker between splash and destination; all three states route correctly; orientation changes during bootstrap don't break routing.
close_reason: Merged in PR #34. SessionState + SessionStateProvider + AuthRepository.signOut + system splash + reactive auth-gated routing all landed. Deferred items (Onboarding, sign-out UI, branded splash) tracked separately.

title: Feed screen composable with pagination + pull-to-refresh
description: LazyColumn of PostCard items; pull-to-refresh via Material3 PullToRefreshBox; append-on-scroll pagination triggered when last visible item index > size - 5. Handles empty and error states.
acceptance_criteria: Scrolling is smooth at 120hz on a capable device; pagination loads additional pages without duplicates; pull-to-refresh resets the list from cursor null.
notes:
Implementation under openspec change add-feature-feed-screen (proposal + design + spec delta on feature-feed + tasks). 'openspec validate add-feature-feed-screen --strict' passes.
Implementation landed on feat/nubecita-1d5-feed-screen-composable-with-pagination-pull-to-ref under openspec change add-feature-feed-screen. PR to follow. Phase 6 (Compose UI tests) deferred to nubecita-1gf (blocked by nubecita-16a). Caught + fixed pre-existing kotlin.time.Instant drift on the way (nubecita-bfz).

title: FeedContract + FeedViewModel (getTimeline)
description: Implement FeedState (posts, isLoading, isRefreshing, cursor, error), FeedEvent (Load, Refresh, LoadMore, OnLikeClicked, ...), FeedEffect (ShowError). ViewModel calls app.bsky.feed.getTimeline via the Hilt-provided atproto client. Maps lexicon output to design-system UI models.
acceptance_criteria: VM unit test covers initial load success/error and pagination cursor advancement.
notes: DTO -> UI model mapper layer placement: default to colocated in :feature:feed:impl until a second consumer (post detail, search) needs the same mappings. Promote to a :core:feed module only when the duplication actually appears, not preemptively. Keep mapper functions pure + total (sealed UI model for unsupported embeds), unit-tested independently of the VM.
close_reason: Landed via PR #43 (squash 3dd7221) — openspec change add-feature-feed-foundation. nubecita-4u5 = FeedContract + FeedViewModel + repository + mapper + Hilt nav wiring + XrpcClientProvider; nubecita-e1a = JUnit 5 + Turbine + MockK + :core:testing migration (subsumed by the test-stack overhaul in the same PR).

title: Build reusable PostCard + primitive composables
description: Add a PostCard composable rendering author avatar, handle, timestamp, text (facet-aware), embed slot, action row (reply/repost/like/share). Plus primitives: Avatar, IconActionButton, FacetText. Stateless — takes UI model + callbacks. Includes @Previews (light/dark/compact/wide).
acceptance_criteria: Components live in :designsystem; previews render; component is consumed by the feed screen; no direct dependency on atproto models (UI model types only).
notes: Implementation in progress under openspec change add-postcard-component (openspec/changes/add-postcard-component/). All 5 prereqs done; PostCard + PostCardShimmer + Modifier.shimmer + supporting embed sub-components shipped in :designsystem; UI models (PostUi/AuthorUi/EmbedUi/ImageUi/PostStatsUi/ViewerStateUi) shipped in new :data:models module per Decision 1. AT Protocol Facet type allowed in :data:models per Decision 2. PostCard is loaded-state-only per Decision 9 (loading via PostCardShimmer, errors via host-screen FeedErrorState). Branch: feat/nubecita-w0d-postcard-component.
close_reason: Merged in PR #42 (commit 2f52dd0). PostCard + PostCardShimmer + Modifier.shimmer + supporting embed sub-components shipped in :designsystem; PostCallbacks data class; UI models (PostUi/AuthorUi/EmbedUi/ImageUi/PostStatsUi/ViewerStateUi) shipped in new :data:models module per Decision 1; AT Protocol Facet allowed per Decision 2; loaded-state-only per Decision 9. Two rounds of Copilot review addressed; openspec change add-postcard-component archived as 2026-04-25-add-postcard-component with delta specs synced (data-models created, design-system updated, +13 reqs).

title: Add MviViewModel unit test harness
description: Verify setState reducer updates uiState and sendEffect delivers one-shot effects exactly once. Use kotlinx-coroutines-test + Turbine.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test class in app/src/test/.../ui/mvi/ passes under ./gradlew testDebugUnitTest.

title: Epic: App shell, splash & onboarding routing
description: Boot experience that decides where the user lands. Uses the Android 12+ SplashScreen API to keep the splash visible while we check auth state, then routes to: onboarding (first install), feed (authed), or login (unauth). Establishes the top-level navigation graph.
design: Single-activity + Navigation Compose. SplashScreen.setKeepOnScreenCondition{} held until auth bootstrap finishes. First-install detection via DataStore flag.
acceptance_criteria: Cold start shows splash until auth is resolved; correct destination reached in each of the three states; no flicker between splash and destination.

title: Epic: Home feed experience
description: First end-to-end social loop screen: the Following timeline. Pulls app.bsky.feed.getTimeline via atproto-kotlin, renders posts using the design system's reusable Post components, supports pagination and pull-to-refresh. Foundation for later HorizontalPager-based feed switcher (Following/Discover/Custom).
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated user sees their Following timeline; scrolling is smooth (60fps min, 120hz target); pagination loads next page on scroll; pull-to-refresh works; failure states are handled.

title: Compose UI tests for FeedScreen (deferred from nubecita-1d5)
description:
Phase 6 of openspec change `add-feature-feed-screen` (which lands the production FeedScreen under nubecita-1d5) wrote unit tests + previews + screenshot tests but deferred the instrumented Compose UI tests because: (a) running them requires a connected emulator/device and `nubecita-16a` (Android instrumented tests via android-emulator-runner) hasn't landed yet, so CI can't execute them; (b) writing ~500 LOC of test code that compiles-but-cannot-run risks landing untested test code.

The OpenSpec spec for `feature-feed` already declares the scenarios these tests would verify: pagination dispatch on threshold crossing, pull-to-refresh dispatch, retry click on the error layout, empty-state rendering, scroll position retention across config-change recreate, scroll position retention across back-nav (push+pop a stub Detail entry through a tiny in-process Nav3 graph mirroring `:app/Navigation.kt`'s decorator setup), and snackbar-replace-not-stack on consecutive ShowError emissions. Tasks.md inside the openspec change lists the exact file names and assertion shapes (section 6, tasks 6.1–6.8).
acceptance_criteria:
- All seven test files added under `feature/feed/impl/src/androidTest/kotlin/...` per openspec change tasks 6.1–6.7.
- A `RecordingFeedViewModel` test double (or equivalent) captures dispatched events for assertion.
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:connectedDebugAndroidTest` green on a 120 Hz emulator.
- The seven spec scenarios under `feature-feed`'s 'Screen ships preview matrix, screenshot tests, and Compose UI tests' requirement (and the back-nav scenario added in the same change) all have a passing test.
notes:
Depends on `nubecita-16a` for automated CI runs, but local development can write + run the tests independently before that lands.

Reference: completed openspec change archive after merge at `openspec/changes/archive/<date>-add-feature-feed-screen/tasks.md` section 6 — names + bodies of each test are spelled out there.

title: Aggregate Jacoco coverage across all Gradle modules
description:
## Why

PR coverage comments from `madrapps/jacoco-report` consistently say "There is no coverage information present for the Files changed" (visible on PRs #41, #42, and #43) because the `jacocoTestReport` task in `app/build.gradle.kts` is scoped to `:app` only:

- `dependsOn("testDebugUnitTest")` — just `:app` tests
- `classDirectories` from `:app/build/tmp/kotlin-classes/debug`
- `executionData` globbed from `:app/build/...`

Most of the codebase lives outside `:app` (`:core:auth`, `:core:common`, `:core:testing`, `:data:models`, `:designsystem`, `:feature:login:impl`, `:feature:feed:api`, `:feature:feed:impl`). Their tests run, their coverage is generated locally, but the aggregated XML the CI action consumes never sees them — so PRs touching those modules show as 0% / "no coverage."

## What needs to happen

Pick one of:

1. **Gradle `jacoco-report-aggregation` plugin** (modern, native) — apply at root, declare module deps as `jacocoAggregation`, single XML at root build dir. Pass that single path to `madrapps/jacoco-report`.

2. **Per-module reports via convention plugin** (Now in Android pattern) — add jacoco wiring to `AndroidLibraryConventionPlugin` so every module emits its own `jacocoTestReport.xml` under `<module>/build/reports/jacoco/...`; pass all XMLs to the action via comma-separated `paths:`.

3. **Quick patch** — extend the existing `:app:jacocoTestReport` to glob `executionData` and `classDirectories` from sibling modules' build dirs. Smallest change but fragile.

Recommend option 2 — it scales as new modules land and matches the existing convention-plugin pattern in `build-logic/`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] CI's coverage comment on a PR touching `:core:*` or `:feature:*` reports a real percentage (not "no coverage information present").
- [ ] `./gradlew jacocoTestReport` (or equivalent root-level task) runs across all modules.
- [ ] No coverage regression in `:app` (the existing report still works).
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` updated to point at the right XML path(s).

## Notes

- `min-coverage-overall` and `min-coverage-changed-files` are currently 0 in `ci.yaml` — leave at 0 until the aggregated baseline is known. Tightening them is a separate decision.
- Screenshot tests don't produce jacoco data (they run on the AGP screenshot runner, not the unit-test source set), so aggregation is unit-test only.

title: Memoize Brush.linearGradient inside Modifier.shimmer to avoid per-frame allocation
description:
Modifier.shimmer's drawWithCache.onDrawBehind currently rebuilds Brush.linearGradient(colors, start, end) on every frame, since start/end are derived from the animating translate value. Brush is a small object but a 60-120hz animation on every shimmered shape adds up — for a feed full of skeletons that's hundreds of brush allocations per second.

Two paths:
  (a) Cache the brush in drawWithCache itself (the cache callback IS already meant for this) — but the brush depends on translate, which changes every frame. Solution: cache the colors list (which is theme-invariant within a recomposition) and only allocate Brush.linearGradient when start/end change.
  (b) Use a custom Shader / Paint via DrawScope.drawIntoCanvas, mutating the shader's transform per frame instead of rebuilding the brush. Lower-level but allocation-free.

Probably (a) — Compose's Brush class is intended to be cheap to construct, but eliminating the  allocation on the hot path is still a win.

Surfaced during the compose-audit pass on PR #42. Profile-driven optimization — only worth it if Macrobenchmark shows shimmer GC pressure at 120Hz on a populated feed.
acceptance_criteria: Macrobenchmark or Perfetto trace shows Modifier.shimmer's per-frame allocation profile reduced. Visual output unchanged from baseline screenshots.

title: Share a single InfiniteTransition across PostCardShimmer placeholders
description:
Each call to Modifier.shimmer() creates its own rememberInfiniteTransition. PostCardShimmer applies the modifier to ~7 shapes (avatar, 3 text bars, optional image rect, 4 action dots), and a feed full of skeletons multiplies that. Result: every shape has an independent shimmer animation that drifts out of sync over time, so a skeleton card looks like a bag of unrelated shimmers rather than one cohesive surface.

Fix shape: lift the InfiniteTransition + animateFloat above the shapes. Either:
  (a) PostCardShimmer creates the transition once and passes a State<Float> to a sibling Modifier.shimmerWithProgress(progress: Float) that paints the brush from the supplied value (no internal animation).
  (b) Provide a CompositionLocal LocalShimmerProgress that Modifier.shimmer() reads when present and falls back to its own rememberInfiniteTransition when not.

(b) keeps the existing one-call API ergonomic for ad-hoc shimmer use; (a) is more explicit. Pick at impl time.

Surfaced during the compose-audit pass on PR #42 (add-postcard-component change). Not perf-critical — the animations themselves are GPU-cheap — purely a visual cohesion polish.
acceptance_criteria: A LazyColumn of 5+ PostCardShimmer instances renders with all internal shapes (and ideally all cards) shimmering at the same brush translate offset. Verifiable by eye in the existing PostCardShimmer screenshot tests (or via a new perceptual-diff check).

title: PostCard recordWithMedia embed (app.bsky.embed.recordWithMedia)
description:
Render Bluesky recordWithMedia embeds: a composition of #record (quoted post) + media (#images or #video). The lexicon's shape is { record: <recordEmbed>, media: <imagesEmbed | videoEmbed> }.

UX shape: media renders above or below the quoted post card. Most clients render media above the quote (matches the visual hierarchy of 'here's an image, and here's the post I'm quoting').

Scope decision context: filed under nubecita-6rh as v3 — explicitly blocked on its constituent parts being supported. Cannot ship before BOTH the record (v2) AND images-or-video tiers exist.

Implementation notes:
- Mapper sealed type adds RecordWithMedia(record: Record, media: Embed) variant — note Embed is the same sealed parent so this composes naturally.
- Renderer is just a Column/Row composition of the existing embed renderers; little new code if the Record + Images/Video ones are well-factored.
- Edge case: media is .video on a recordWithMedia — defer until the standalone video tier ships.

Decision doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-postcard-embed-scope-v1.md
acceptance_criteria: RecordWithMedia(record, media) variant added to embed sealed type; composes existing PostCardRecordEmbed + (PostCardImageEmbed | PostCardVideoEmbed); manual smoke on a real recordWithMedia post via Bluesky API.

title: PostCard video embed (app.bsky.embed.video)
description:
Render Bluesky video clip embeds in PostCard. Schema (per app.bsky.embed.video lexicon): a single video clip with an .m3u8 HLS playlist URL, alt text, aspect ratio, and a thumbnail blob.

UX shape: video tile (thumbnail-then-play) with tap-to-play inline OR tap-to-open-in-detail. Inline play needs scroll-aware lifecycle (pause when scrolled offscreen, release ExoPlayer when far from viewport).

Scope decision context: filed under nubecita-6rh as v3 — lands after PostCard v1 (text+images), v1.1 (external), and v2 (record). Multi-day work due to Media3/ExoPlayer integration. Block until a video-embed test post in our feed is identifiable as a real UX gap, not a long-tail rarity.

Open decision (filed separately if needed): Media3 (androidx.media3:media3-exoplayer + media3-ui) vs raw ExoPlayer — Media3 is current and recommended; raw ExoPlayer is deprecated path. Default to Media3 unless something in atproto-kotlin or our feed pipeline forces otherwise.

Implementation notes:
- Mapper sealed type adds Video(playlistUrl, thumbUrl, aspectRatio, altText) variant.
- ExoPlayer instance lifecycle is the hard problem — best practice is one player per visible item with LazyListState awareness. AndroidView{ PlayerView(...) } for the surface.
- HLS playlist (.m3u8) requires media3-exoplayer-hls module.
- Audio: muted by default (matches Bluesky web client behavior); tap to unmute.

Decision doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-postcard-embed-scope-v1.md
acceptance_criteria: Video(...) variant added to embed sealed type; Media3 ExoPlayer integration ships in :designsystem (or :feature:feed:impl — decide); inline thumbnail-then-play pattern with scroll-aware pause/release; muted-by-default; manual smoke on a real video post via Bluesky API.

title: Relative-time formatter for post / profile timestamps
description:
Posts, profile join dates, and (later) notifications display times relative to now: "now", "2m", "4h", "5d", then absolute date ("Mar 15") past some threshold (typically a week).

Need:
- A pure helper: (now: Instant, then: Instant) -> String. Deterministic, easily unit-testable.
- A Composable wrapper that recomposes periodically while visible so a "now" post becomes "1m" without scroll. Use a slow ticker (e.g. flow that emits every 30s) consumed via collectAsStateWithLifecycle, or rememberCoroutineScope + delay loop.
- AT Protocol records carry RFC 3339 strings (createdAt). Helper accepts kotlinx.datetime.Instant; the DTO->UI mapper handles the parse.

Lives in :core:common (cross-cutting; not Compose-specific in the core helper, only the auto-recomposing wrapper is) so non-feed surfaces (search results, notifications) can reuse.

Blocks PostCard (nubecita-w0d) — every post displays a timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Pure formatter helper unit-tested against fixed (now, then) pairs covering second/minute/hour/day/week/absolute-date thresholds; Composable wrapper updates without scroll-triggered recomposition of the whole list; helper uses kotlinx-datetime.
close_reason: Merged in PR #37 (commit cb589f0). Pure helper formatRelativeTime + Composable rememberRelativeTimeText shipped in :core:common with 18 unit tests. Localized via Android string + plural resources (RelativeTimeStrings.English fixture for tests). i18n + lint + Copilot review + compose-audit feedback all addressed in follow-up commits before merge.

title: Archive add-mvi-foundation openspec change
description: PR #15 merged today; move openspec/changes/add-mvi-foundation/ into openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-21-add-mvi-foundation/ per the openspec-archive-change workflow. Keeps openspec/changes/ reflecting only in-flight work.
close_reason: Archived in commit 021c6a6 (add-mvi-foundation + add-designsystem-theme batched cleanup, post-PR #34)

title: Document SavedStateHandle pattern for MVI VMs
description: add-mvi-foundation (nubecita-aew) explicitly excluded SavedStateHandle / process-death persistence from the MviViewModel base class — screens that need it inject SavedStateHandle via Hilt per-VM. Park this issue until the first screen actually requires survival across process death (likely compose/draft or feed scroll position). When it does, capture the pattern in CLAUDE.md conventions + a short worked example.

title: Onboarding screen for first install
description: Lightweight 2-3 page intro (value prop, feeds, privacy). HorizontalPager. On finish, set firstInstallComplete=true in DataStore and route to login.
acceptance_criteria: Shown only on first install; dismissible; writing the flag survives process death; skipped on subsequent cold starts.

title: Replace .weight(1f).fillMaxSize() with .weight(1f).fillMaxHeight() in PostCardImageEmbed
description:
PostCardImageEmbed's TwoImages/ThreeImages/FourImages helpers chain Modifier.weight(1f).fillMaxSize() on Row children. weight(1f) already takes the available space along the parent's flex axis (width for Row, height for Column), so fillMaxSize() is only adding the cross-axis fill — which fillMaxHeight() (or fillMaxWidth() for Column children) expresses more precisely.

Pure semantic readability cleanup. No behavioral change — the rendered layout is identical.

Surfaced during the compose-audit pass on PR #42. Backlog quality work; pick up alongside any other PostCardImageEmbed change.
acceptance_criteria: Modifier chains in PostCardImageEmbed use .weight(1f).fillMaxHeight() (Row children) or .weight(1f).fillMaxWidth() (Column children). Existing screenshot baselines unchanged.

value: Auth strategy is OAuth-only. App-password path was dropped on 2026-04-24; nubecita-ym3 closed as superseded by nubecita-ck0. Session storage (nubecita-nss) persists a single OAuthSession as defined by the atproto-oauth library. Epic nubecita-gr4 title still reads 'app password + OAuth' but scope is OAuth-only — retitle when next touched.
